# <yrChapterNumber>28.1 </yrChapterNumber> SQL Practice: Movies Table

## Download the practice data

Use either format below (same data in both files):

- SQLite database file: [moviesPractice-v001.sqlite](data/moviesPractice-v001.sqlite)
- CSV file: [moviesPractice-v001.csv](data/moviesPractice-v001.csv)

The SQLite file contains one table named `movies`.

## Table structure

`movies(movie_id, title, genre, director, release_year, runtime_minutes, rating, domestic_box_office_millions, oscar_wins, franchise)`

## Sample rows

| movie_id | title | genre | director | release_year | runtime_minutes | rating | domestic_box_office_millions | oscar_wins | franchise |
|---:|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---:|---|
| 1 | The Last Algorithm | Sci-Fi | Ava Chen | 2018 | 124 | PG-13 | 210.4 | 2 | DataVerse |
| 6 | Skyline Detectives II | Action | Noah Price | 2022 | 146 | PG-13 | 455.1 | 2 | Skyline |
| 11 | Map of Small Worlds | Animation | Kira Stone | 2018 | 93 | G | 132.4 | 1 | Small Worlds |
| 16 | The Null Value | Mystery | Priya Raman | 2024 | 110 | NULL | NULL | 0 | NULL |

## Practice questions (easy to challenging)

Write SQL queries that answer the following.

### Level 1: Core SELECT/FROM

1. Show all columns for all movies.
2. Show only `title`, `director`, and `release_year`.
3. Show the first 5 rows in alphabetical order by `title`.
4. Show all distinct `genre` values in alphabetical order.

### Level 2: WHERE, ORDER BY, LIMIT, IN, BETWEEN

5. Show all movies released between 2018 and 2022 (inclusive), ordered by `release_year` then `title`.
6. Show all movies directed by `Ava Chen`, `Noah Price`, or `Lena Brooks`.
7. Show the 3 longest movies (display `title` and `runtime_minutes`).
8. Show movies with `rating = 'PG'` or `rating = 'G'`, ordered by `release_year` descending.

### Level 3: LIKE, calculated columns, aliases

9. Show movies whose `title` starts with `Map`.
10. Show movies whose `title` contains the word `Skyline`.
11. Show `title`, `runtime_minutes`, and a calculated column named `runtime_hours` (`runtime_minutes / 60.0`).
12. Show `title` and a calculated column named `box_office_per_minute` (`domestic_box_office_millions / runtime_minutes`), ordered from highest to lowest.

### Level 4: NULL handling and logic

13. Show movies where `rating` is NULL.
14. Show movies where `domestic_box_office_millions` is NOT NULL and is at least 100.
15. Show movies where `franchise` is NULL and `release_year >= 2018`.
16. Show movies where `genre = 'Drama'` and (`release_year < 2018` or `oscar_wins >= 1`).

### Level 5: GROUP BY and HAVING

17. For each `director`, show the number of movies they directed (`movie_count`), ordered from largest to smallest.
18. For each `genre`, show average runtime (`avg_runtime`) rounded to 1 decimal place.
19. Show directors with at least 3 movies in the table.
20. For each non-NULL `franchise`, show total domestic box office and keep only franchises above 300 million.

## Optional stretch questions

21. Show the second page of results when listing movies by `title`, 5 rows per page.
22. Show each movie with a label column named `era` that is:
   - `'classic'` if `release_year < 2015`
   - `'modern'` if `release_year` is 2015 to 2020
   - `'new'` if `release_year > 2020`
23. Show the top movie (or movies) by `domestic_box_office_millions` within each `genre`.
